result.summary: Kaleyra exposes documented HTTP APIs for its CPaaS platform plus a separate Voice API; both require a Kaleyra-issued API key. No public GraphQL API, MCP server, or CLI was found.
result.description: Kaleyra provides communications-platform services for businesses, including SMS, WhatsApp, voice, email, video, verification, and related messaging tools. Its public developer documentation is hosted on developers.kaleyra.io, with separate voice API docs on apidocs-voice.kaleyra.com.

kaleyra_api_key.label: Kaleyra API key
kaleyra_api_key.setup: Sign in to your [Kaleyra.io account](https://www.kaleyra.io/), then open [Create Kaleyra.io API Key](https://developers.kaleyra.io/docs/generating-an-api-key). In the platform, go to **Developers → API Keys** and generate a new key. Kaleyra's getting-started docs also point to [View API Key and SID](https://developers.kaleyra.io/docs/view-api-key-and-sid) after creation.

surfaces.name: Kaleyra Cloud APIs
surfaces.notes: Developer hub lists SMS, Consent, Video REST API v2, Email REST APIs, Blocklist, Document, Block User, and WhatsApp Payment APIs under a common API Reference. The scraped reference pages did not expose a machine-readable OpenAPI URL or a single shared base URL.

surfaces.name: Kaleyra Voice API
surfaces.notes: Interactive voice docs list operations such as Click-to-Call, call status, call details, blacklisting, follow-ups, OBD, and health status. The docs mention `api_key` as a parameter but the example request binds it via the `x-api-key` header.
